stemHtml: Which statement correctly defines a Runway End Safety Area (RESA)?

options.a: An area symmetrical about the extended runway centreline, adjacent to the end of the strip, primarily intended to reduce the risk of damage to an aircraft undershooting or overrunning the runway.
options.b: A defined area including the runway and any stopway, intended to reduce the risk of damage to aircraft running off the runway and to protect aircraft during take-off or landing.
options.c: A defined rectangular area at the runway end prepared as a suitable area in which an aircraft may be stopped during an abandoned take-off.
options.d: A defined rectangular area on the ground or water under appropriate authority, selected as a suitable area over which an aircraft may make a portion of its initial climb to a specified height.
